message:
fix: connectivity / Eulerian predicates handle self-loops (br-r37-c1-792dv)

Four parity defects on graphs with self-loops, all rooted in the
Rust impl ignoring the +2 degree contribution that a self-loop
makes in nx's calculations:

1. ``edge_connectivity(Graph([(1,1)]))``      fnx: 0   nx: 2
2. ``node_connectivity(Graph([(1,1)]))``      fnx: 0   nx: 2
3. ``has_eulerian_path(Graph([(1,1)]))``      fnx: False  nx: True
4. ``is_eulerian(Graph([(1,2),(2,3),(3,1),(1,1)]))``  fnx: False  nx: True

For multi-node graphs the gap is just as wide:
- ``edge_connectivity(Graph([(1,1),(1,2),(2,2)]))``  fnx: 1  nx: 3
- ``node_connectivity(...)``                          fnx: 2  nx: 3

nx's algorithms count the self-loop's +2 degree contribution
(undirected; +1 for in/out on directed) which (a) lifts the
min-degree bound used by edge/node_connectivity in the
"complete graph" branch, and (b) keeps every node's degree even
when the self-loop is balanced, which makes self-loop-augmented
Eulerian graphs detectable. The Rust fast path either ignores
self-loops or special-cases 1-node graphs.

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``: when the input graph
contains at least one self-loop, delegate to nx for:
- ``edge_connectivity``
- ``node_connectivity``
- ``has_eulerian_path``
- ``is_eulerian`` (only when ``number_of_nodes() > 1``; the
  existing 0/1-node Python branch already handles single-node-
  with-self-loop correctly)

The Rust fast path remains the default for the (vastly more
common) self-loop-free inputs, so performance is unchanged for
the typical case.

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_self_loop_connectivity_eulerian_parity.py``:
- test_single_node_self_loop_connectivity_matches_nx
  (parametrized over int/string nodes; both connectivities = 2)
- test_single_node_self_loop_eulerian_predicates_match_nx
  (both predicates True)
- test_K3_plus_self_loop_eulerian_matches_nx
  (triangle + self-loop: still Eulerian)
- test_two_self_loops_plus_bridge_connectivity_matches_nx
  (the multi-node 3-vs-1/2 divergence regression)
- test_self_loop_plus_chain_eulerian_path_matches_nx
  (1→1→2→3 path has an Eulerian path)
- test_no_self_loop_fast_path_unchanged (parametrized over
  path_graph / cycle_graph / complete_graph: regression guard
  for the Rust fast path)
- test_disconnected_graph_with_self_loops_still_returns_zero_connectivity
  (a self-loop in one component doesn't promote whole-graph
  connectivity past 0 on disconnected input)

Full sweep
----------
6669 passed, 0 failed (was 6656 before — +13 new regression cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -33,6 +33,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-6yimw","title":"node_degree_xy iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.node_degree_xy yields tuples in G.nodes()-insertion order, but nx iterates node_set in hash order (since the implementation does set(G) and iterates that). The same 10 (degree_x, degree_y) pairs are produced; only the iteration order differs.

Repro:
  edges = [(c,d),(a,b),(b,c),(d,e),(a,c)]
  fnx -> [(3,2),(3,2),(3,2),(2,3),(2,1),(2,2),(2,3),(2,2),(2,3),(1,2)]
  nx  -> [(2,2),(2,3),(2,3),(2,1),(3,2),(3,2),(3,2),(2,2),(2,3),(1,2)]

  Sorted: both produce identical multisets of pairs.

Drop-in code that compared the iterator output to a reference list broke. Fix: replicate nx's exact algorithm (iterate xdeg(set(nodes)) which gives hash-set order).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:13:04.535552901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:16:59.265769717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:16:59.263711058Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-71glx","title":"parity: 3 signature mismatches (gaussian_random_partition_graph arg order, find_minimal_d_separator wrong sig, to_nested_tuple leaks _parent)","description":"Three signature-parity defects discovered while signature-diffing fnx __all__ vs upstream nx (excluding backend dispatch noise). (1) gaussian_random_partition_graph: nx is (n, s, v, p_in, p_out, directed=False, seed=None) but fnx is (n, s, v, p_in, p_out, seed=None, directed=False) — positional arg order swapped, breaks calls like nx.gaussian_random_partition_graph(10, 5, 0.1, 0.4, 0.05, True). (2) find_minimal_d_separator: nx is (G, x, y, *, included=None, restricted=None) but fnx is (G, u, v) — wrong param names AND missing keyword-only included/restricted. (3) to_nested_tuple: fnx leaks an internal _parent=None recursion arg in its public signature. Fix: align signatures; for find_minimal_d_separator, route through nx via _call_networkx_for_parity to handle the new kwargs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:49:37.606667646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:38.730801947Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:38.728883324Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 21b0340: 3 signature fixes (gaussian_random_partition_graph arg order, find_minimal_d_separator delegated to nx with included/restricted kwargs, to_nested_tuple no longer leaks _parent). Smoke test still passes 170/170.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-75r94","title":"parity: coloring strategy_* (8) + matching_dict_to_set (1) wrappers","description":"Two clusters of nx utilities not surfaced. Coloring strategies (used as strategy= argument to greedy_color): strategy_largest_first(G, colors), strategy_random_sequential(G, colors, seed=None), strategy_smallest_last(G, colors), strategy_independent_set(G, colors), strategy_connected_sequential_bfs(G, colors), strategy_connected_sequential_dfs(G, colors), strategy_connected_sequential(G, colors, traversal=bfs), strategy_saturation_largest_first(G, colors). Matching utility: matching_dict_to_set(matching) — converts a {node: partner} dict to a set of (u, v) frozensets. Add Python wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:29:37.916763877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T23:44:39.656055329Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T23:44:39.653993535Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 7939284: 8 strategy_* coloring callables + matching_dict_to_set. Verified with nx.greedy_color(C6, strategy=fnx.strategy_largest_first) matching nx output. Follow-up: fnx.greedy_color does not accept callable strategies (only strings) — separate parity gap.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-792dv","title":"edge_connectivity/node_connectivity/has_eulerian_path drift on single-node-with-self-loop","description":"On a graph with exactly one node and a single self-loop edge, three connectivity-related functions diverge from nx:

  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1, 1)])
  >>> fnx.edge_connectivity(g)         # 0   (nx returns 2)
  >>> fnx.node_connectivity(g)         # 0   (nx returns 2)
  >>> fnx.has_eulerian_path(g)         # False (nx returns True)

nx's edge_connectivity/node_connectivity for a single-node graph fall through to the 'complete graph' branch which returns min_degree — and a node with a self-loop has degree 2 in undirected graphs, so nx returns 2.

nx's has_eulerian_path on single-node-with-self-loop returns True because the self-loop itself constitutes a valid (degenerate) Eulerian path.

fnx's Rust _raw_node_connectivity / _raw_edge_connectivity return 0 for this case, and _raw_has_eulerian_path returns False. All three are incorrect relative to nx.

Other single-node configurations are correct: a single isolated node yields ec=nc=0 and has_eulerian_path=True on both sides; two-node and larger graphs match.

Fix: when len(G) == 1 AND the node has a self-loop, delegate edge_connectivity/node_connectivity/has_eulerian_path to nx. The Rust fast path remains the default for all other shapes.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T06:11:45.784349360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T06:12:17.141416439Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-7u6up","title":"parity: community edge_betweenness_partition + greedy_source_expansion missing","description":"Two community-detection helpers from networkx.algorithms.community not exposed by franken_networkx: edge_betweenness_partition(G, number_of_sets, weight=None) and greedy_source_expansion(G, source, ...). Add Python wrappers delegating via _call_networkx_community_for_parity, export at top-level and add to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T12:13:47.981273951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T13:05:50.852667948Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T13:05:50.850778351Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4eb0d6d: edge_betweenness_partition + greedy_source_expansion verified on karate","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-7zrd7","title":"parity: geometric graph generators missing kwargs (pos_name, weight_name, metric, p, p_dist, weight)","description":"Four geometric-graph generators had stale signatures vs nx: soft_random_geometric_graph (missing p, pos_name); waxman_graph (metric, pos_name); geographical_threshold_graph (metric, p_dist, pos_name, weight_name); thresholded_random_geometric_graph (weight, p, pos_name, weight_name). Drop-in callers passing any of those kwargs hit TypeError. Aligned all 4 signatures to nx exactly and implemented the new behavior: Minkowski p-norm distances, custom metric callable, configurable pos/weight attribute names, p_dist scoring fn, weight as callable/dict/scalar.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:15:43.861443164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:16:32.381315774Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:16:32.379452394Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit a3feb2d. All 4 geometric generators now match nx signatures + honour p/metric/pos_name/weight_name/p_dist/weight kwargs with proper Minkowski p-norm distance helper. 22 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-8hshk","title":"equivalence_classes returns list instead of nx's set (and wrong iter order)","description":"fnx.equivalence_classes returns a list of frozensets in iteration order (a, b, c, d, e), while nx returns a set of frozensets (which iterates in Python hash order). The return type and iteration order differ.

Repro:
  nodes = ['a','b','c','d','e']; eq = lambda x,y: x == y
  fnx -> [frozenset({'a'}), frozenset({'b'}), ...] (list, input order)
  nx  -> {frozenset({'d'}), frozenset({'a'}), frozenset({'b'}), ...} (set, hash order)
  
  list(nx.equivalence_classes(...)) yields hash-order frozensets.
  list(fnx.equivalence_classes(...)) yields input-order frozensets.

Drop-in code expecting nx's set-of-frozensets return type or its hash-based iteration order broke.

Fix: delegate to nx (small function, simple delegation). Mirrors the cycle_basis / find_cliques / SCC delegation pattern.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:15:26.239493580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:17:56.538339706Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:17:56.536238627Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -3822,6 +3822,15 @@ def node_connectivity(G, s=None, t=None, flow_func=None):
             t=t,
             flow_func=flow_func,
         )
+    # br-r37-c1-792dv: the Rust _raw_node_connectivity ignores the
+    # +2 degree contribution that self-loops make in nx's
+    # min-degree-bound calculation. On a single-node graph with a
+    # self-loop nx returns 2 vs fnx's 0; on a 2-node graph with two
+    # self-loops + a bridge nx returns 3 (min_degree=3) vs fnx's 2.
+    # Delegate any graph with at least one self-loop to nx so both
+    # libraries agree on the self-loop weighting.
+    if any(u == v for u, v in G.edges()):
+        return _call_networkx_for_parity("node_connectivity", G, s=s, t=t)
     # Multigraphs with parallel edges bump the min-degree bound above
     # the simple-graph κ = n−1 ceiling (a node with k parallel edges to a
     # neighbour contributes k to its degree). networkx uses that bound
@@ -3875,6 +3884,17 @@ def edge_connectivity(G, s=None, t=None, flow_func=None, cutoff=None):
             flow_func=flow_func,
             cutoff=cutoff,
         )
+    # br-r37-c1-792dv: the Rust _raw_edge_connectivity ignores the
+    # +2 degree contribution that self-loops make in nx's
+    # min-degree-bound calculation. nx's value on graphs with any
+    # self-loop is generally larger than fnx's (e.g. single-node
+    # self-loop: nx=2 vs fnx=0; two-node + two self-loops + bridge:
+    # nx=3 vs fnx=1). Delegate any self-loop graph to nx so both
+    # libraries agree on the weighting.
+    if any(u == v for u, v in G.edges()):
+        return _call_networkx_for_parity(
+            "edge_connectivity", G, s=s, t=t, cutoff=cutoff
+        )
     # Multigraphs: the Rust implementation collapses parallel edges and
     # returns the simple-graph edge-connectivity (and as a float), whereas
     # networkx counts parallel edges and returns an int. Delegate to keep
@@ -5302,6 +5322,13 @@ def is_eulerian(G):
             if deg % 2 != 0:
                 return False
         return True
+    # br-r37-c1-792dv: the Rust _raw_is_eulerian also mishandles
+    # self-loops on multi-node graphs (e.g. K3 + self-loop on one
+    # vertex remains Eulerian per nx but the Rust path returns
+    # False). Delegate to nx whenever any self-loop is present so
+    # both predicates carry the same self-loop semantics.
+    if any(u == v for u, v in G.edges()):
+        return _call_networkx_for_parity("is_eulerian", G)
     return _raw_is_eulerian(G)
 
 
@@ -5390,6 +5417,14 @@ def has_eulerian_path(G, source=None):
         return _call_networkx_for_parity("has_eulerian_path", G, source=source)
     if source is not None:
         return _call_networkx_for_parity("has_eulerian_path", G, source=source)
+    # br-r37-c1-792dv: the Rust _raw_has_eulerian_path mishandles
+    # self-loops (each self-loop adds 2 to degree, keeping parity
+    # even). On graphs with self-loops the Rust path returns False
+    # where nx returns True (e.g. K3 + self-loop, single-node
+    # self-loop, self-loop attached to a chain). Delegate any
+    # graph with at least one self-loop to nx.
+    if any(u == v for u, v in G.edges()):
+        return _call_networkx_for_parity("has_eulerian_path", G, source=source)
     return _raw_has_eulerian_path(G)
 
 # Algorithm functions — paths and cycles

@@ -0,0 +1,131 @@
+"""Parity for connectivity / Eulerian predicates on graphs with self-loops.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-792dv. The Rust _raw_node_connectivity,
+_raw_edge_connectivity, _raw_has_eulerian_path, and _raw_is_eulerian
+all silently mishandled self-loops:
+
+  - edge/node_connectivity ignored the +2 degree contribution that
+    a self-loop makes in nx's min-degree-bound calculation. Examples:
+      * fnx.edge_connectivity(Graph([(1,1)]))         -> 0  (nx: 2)
+      * fnx.node_connectivity(Graph([(1,1)]))         -> 0  (nx: 2)
+      * fnx.edge_connectivity(Graph([(1,1),(1,2),(2,2)])) -> 1  (nx: 3)
+      * fnx.node_connectivity(...)                   -> 2  (nx: 3)
+
+  - has_eulerian_path / is_eulerian returned False on graphs where
+    the self-loops kept all degrees even but the helper either
+    ignored self-loops or special-cased 1-node graphs:
+      * fnx.has_eulerian_path(Graph([(1,2),(2,3),(3,1),(1,1)])) -> False  (nx: True)
+      * fnx.is_eulerian(...)                                    -> False  (nx: True)
+
+The fix delegates these four functions to nx whenever the graph has
+at least one self-loop, leaving the Rust fast path intact for the
+common no-self-loop case.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Single-node graph with a self-loop
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("node", [0, 1, "x"])
+def test_single_node_self_loop_connectivity_matches_nx(node):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(node, node)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(node, node)])
+    assert fnx.edge_connectivity(G) == nx.edge_connectivity(GX) == 2
+    assert fnx.node_connectivity(G) == nx.node_connectivity(GX) == 2
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("node", [0, 1, "x"])
+def test_single_node_self_loop_eulerian_predicates_match_nx(node):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(node, node)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(node, node)])
+    assert fnx.has_eulerian_path(G) is nx.has_eulerian_path(GX) is True
+    assert fnx.is_eulerian(G) is nx.is_eulerian(GX) is True
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Multi-node graphs with self-loops
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_K3_plus_self_loop_eulerian_matches_nx():
+    """A triangle plus one self-loop: still Eulerian under nx (all
+    degrees even when self-loop counts +2). Pre-fix fnx returned
+    False for both predicates."""
+    edges = [(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1), (1, 1)]
+    G = fnx.Graph(edges)
+    GX = nx.Graph(edges)
+    assert fnx.has_eulerian_path(G) is nx.has_eulerian_path(GX) is True
+    assert fnx.is_eulerian(G) is nx.is_eulerian(GX) is True
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_two_self_loops_plus_bridge_connectivity_matches_nx():
+    """Two-node graph: self-loop on each node + a bridge between
+    them. Each node has degree 3 (1 from self-loop ×2 + 1 from
+    bridge). nx returns 3 (min-degree bound on the complete graph
+    branch); fnx pre-fix returned 1 for edge and 2 for node
+    connectivity."""
+    edges = [(1, 1), (1, 2), (2, 2)]
+    G = fnx.Graph(edges)
+    GX = nx.Graph(edges)
+    assert fnx.edge_connectivity(G) == nx.edge_connectivity(GX) == 3
+    assert fnx.node_connectivity(G) == nx.node_connectivity(GX) == 3
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_self_loop_plus_chain_eulerian_path_matches_nx():
+    """Self-loop on node 1 attached to a 1→2→3 chain. nx says
+    has_eulerian_path=True (1→1→2→3 traverses all edges once);
+    pre-fix fnx returned False because the helper miscounted
+    odd-degree vertices."""
+    edges = [(1, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3)]
+    G = fnx.Graph(edges)
+    GX = nx.Graph(edges)
+    assert fnx.has_eulerian_path(G) is nx.has_eulerian_path(GX) is True
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Regression guard for the no-self-loop fast path
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("graph_factory_name", ["path_graph", "cycle_graph", "complete_graph"])
+def test_no_self_loop_fast_path_unchanged(graph_factory_name):
+    """The Rust fast path remains the default when no self-loop is
+    present — make sure the new self-loop guard doesn't accidentally
+    capture clean graphs."""
+    G = getattr(fnx, graph_factory_name)(5)
+    GX = getattr(nx, graph_factory_name)(5)
+    assert fnx.edge_connectivity(G) == nx.edge_connectivity(GX)
+    assert fnx.node_connectivity(G) == nx.node_connectivity(GX)
+    assert fnx.has_eulerian_path(G) == nx.has_eulerian_path(GX)
+    assert fnx.is_eulerian(G) == nx.is_eulerian(GX)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_disconnected_graph_with_self_loops_still_returns_zero_connectivity():
+    """Drop-in: a self-loop in one component does not promote the
+    whole-graph connectivity past zero on a disconnected input.
+    Both fnx and nx return 0 (and has_eulerian_path=False)."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 1), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 1), (2, 3)])
+    assert fnx.edge_connectivity(G) == nx.edge_connectivity(GX) == 0
+    assert fnx.node_connectivity(G) == nx.node_connectivity(GX) == 0
+    assert fnx.has_eulerian_path(G) is nx.has_eulerian_path(GX) is False
